postCallbackSequence: sign canonical challenge with non-exportable product P-256 key
callbackTransport.securityBoundary: The callback approval is short-lived and has no product session or asset authority. Session creation requires a fresh central challenge and proof from the exact product device key.

productSessionProof.verification: Decode the proof and call CanonicalWalletGatewayAdapter.introspect with the HTTP method, exact raw body digest and signed product path. Treat the path header as untrusted until the proof verifies, then require a fixed one-to-one mapping from /api/authority/* to the current /app/resource-market/* route before executing authority logic.
productSessionProof.replay: Consume each proof once in the central adapter. The product proxy also persists a bounded replay set before forwarding.
productSessionProof.legacyHeaders: reject Authorization bearer session binding and X-YNX-Product-Device-Key

reason: Registry, Gateway routes and authoritative Resource routes are not merged or deployed in the central worktree. Product adapter fails closed and never infers settlement.
